heading: Compensation by common carriers
label: 40904 Compensation by common carriers
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)4090446 App.:1718(e)., , ; , , .Pub. L. 98–237, § 19(e)98 Stat. 88Pub. L. 105–258, title I, § 116112 Stat. 1912Mar. 20, 1984Oct. 14, 1998In this section, the words “ocean freight forwarder” are substituted for “ocean transportation intermediary, as defined in section 1702(17)(A) of this Appendix” and “ocean transportation intermediary” because the definition of “ocean transportation intermediary” in section 1702(17)(A) contains a definition of “ocean freight forwarder” which is restated as a separate definition.In subsection (d)(1), the word “calendar” is omitted as unnecessary.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)4090446 App.:1718(e)., , ; , , .Pub. L. 98–237, § 19(e)98 Stat. 88Pub. L. 105–258, title I, § 116112 Stat. 1912Mar. 20, 1984Oct. 14, 1998In this section, the words “ocean freight forwarder” are substituted for “ocean transportation intermediary, as defined in section 1702(17)(A) of this Appendix” and “ocean transportation intermediary” because the definition of “ocean transportation intermediary” in section 1702(17)(A) contains a definition of “ocean freight forwarder” which is restated as a separate definition.In subsection (d)(1), the word “calendar” is omitted as unnecessary.

children.text: engaged, booked, secured, reserved, or contracted directly with the carrier or its agent for space aboard a vessel or confirmed the availability of the space; and
prepared and processed the ocean bill of lading, dock receipt, or other similar document for the shipment.

children.heading: .—Dual Compensation
children.label: b .—Dual Compensation
children.text: A common carrier may not pay compensation for services described in subsection (a) more than once on the same shipment.

children.heading: .—Beneficial Interest Shipments
children.label: c .—Beneficial Interest Shipments
children.text: An ocean freight forwarder may not receive compensation from a common carrier for a shipment in which the ocean freight forwarder has a direct or indirect beneficial interest. A common carrier may not knowingly pay compensation on that shipment.

children.heading: .—Limits on Authority of Conference or Group
children.label: d .—Limits on Authority of Conference or Group

children.text: deny a member of the conference or group the right, upon notice of not more than 5 days, to take independent action on any level of compensation paid to an ocean freight forwarder; or

agree to limit the payment of compensation to an ocean freight forwarder to less than 1.25 percent of the aggregate of all rates and charges applicable under a tariff and assessed against the cargo on which the services of the ocean freight forwarder are provided.
